summary:
I've written the technical design to `.pipeliner/phases/02-plan/main/design-writer/output/design.md`.

**What I did:** Read the Define-phase requirements (R1–R34), discovery notes, and open questions from the Define phase, then read the actual current codebase (`Phase`, `Pipeline`, `Step`, `StepRun` models; `ManagerTick`, `Approve`, `SendBack`, `AnswerQuestions`, `StepRuns::Queue/Complete/Claim/Sweep` services; controllers, routes, views, helpers) to ground every design decision in real code rather than assumptions.

**Key design decisions:**
- A new `Phase.status` value `"paused"`, which `ManagerTick`'s existing `unless @phase.running?` guard already excludes for free.
- Two new booleans (`pause_requested`, `restart_in_progress`) plus a `restart_feedback` jsonb column handle the "hold after in-flight step finishes" and "multi-step restart cascade" cases, reusing `ManagerTick`'s existing dispatch/route/converge engine rather than inventing a new one.
- A new read-only `Phases::Convergence` PORO (extracted from `ManagerTick`) lets "Done" (`Phases::Approve`) check live whether a paused phase is actually settled, since paused phases aren't ticked.
- Explore/Clarifying Questions menu items resolve their target step by declared output artifact (`discovery_notes`/`open_questions`), not by slug, since slugs vary per project template.
- Two new services (`Phases::Pause`, `Phases::RerunMenuStep`, `Phases::RestartDefine`) plus small additive extensions to `Approve` and `AnswerQuestions` — all existing gate behavior (R32–R34) is untouched.

The doc includes full code sketches for every service/model/controller/view change, a complete R1–R34 traceability table, and a file-level plan of new/modified/verified-unchanged files.
